title: Extend archive-old-reports.js to cover narrative-cite-audit-*.json and verify-pending-run-*.json (clone-side disk-bloat fix)
do_not_implement: DO NOT modify build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js, the FUSE workspace, or any scheduled task without operator authorization. PROP outlines the proposed change; operator decides scope (rotation only, compaction, or skip-on-no-change), retention window, and whether/how to address the FUSE-mount component.
summary: monitor/integrity/narrative-cite-audit-<ts>.json files are produced hourly by workspace-sync's audit-narrative-citations.js, weigh ~1.5MB each, and are never archived. As of 2026-05-14T02:42Z the directory holds 253 such files in git (386MB total) and 244 in FUSE (363MB). Growth rate ~36MB/day / ~1.1GB/month. The companion verify-pending-run-<ts>.json is also hourly but tiny (~1.6KB) so its accumulation cost is structural-clutter only (244 files). The existing build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js was designed for exactly this class of problem (per-timestamp append-only run reports) and ALREADY targets monitor/integrity, but its TARGETS regex is `/^report-.*\.json$/` — which doesn't match either `narrative-cite-audit-*` or `verify-pending-run-*`. Extending the regex is a one-line fix that recovers ~370MB of clone-side bloat per run cycle and bounds future growth. Note: the same script's header documents that the FUSE mount cannot unlink, so this PROP only fixes the GIT side; the FUSE side stays unrecoverable until operator does manual file-tool cleanup or a workspace-sync mechanism is built (outside this PROP's scope).

background.trigger: Mode 2 disk audit during tinker run 2026-05-14T02-40 detected project footprint = 1014MB (>1000MB CRITICAL threshold). Decomposition: 560MB FUSE workspace + 450MB /tmp/edit-clone + ~5MB other. Within FUSE, monitor/integrity/ accounts for 446MB of the 560MB total, dominated by 244 narrative-cite-audit files at 1.6MB each. The /tmp/edit-clone is operator's working clone and ineligible for tinker cleanup (4h old, < 24h policy threshold). The narrative-cite accumulation is the largest unmanaged disk grower in the pipeline.
background.discovery_path: Tinker Mode 2 Step 1 (FUSE check passed) → Step 2 (auth passed) → disk audit caught 1014MB footprint → drill-down on monitor/integrity revealed 244-file narrative-cite-audit pile → confirmed via GitHub API that git mirrors the same accumulation (253 files, 386MB).

current_archival.gap: monitor/integrity TARGETS regex `/^report-.*\.json$/` matches the ZERO files currently named `report-*` in that directory (the 44 'report-' files in git are PRE-MAY 4 leftovers). The 253 narrative-cite-audit files and 253 verify-pending-run files — the actual accumulation surface — fall outside the regex.
current_archival.invocation: Not currently scheduled. Script header says 'Usage: node build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js [--apply --days N]'. Manual operator invocation only. No cron entry, no agent trigger.

growth_rate.verify_pending_run: ~1.6KB/hour = 38KB/day = 1.2MB/month (file count, not byte size, is the issue)
growth_rate.projected_clone_bloat_no_action: If left unchanged, monitor/integrity will pass 1GB in git by mid-June 2026 and 5GB by November 2026, materially slowing every clone (decider, curmudgeon, edit-clone) — 5GB clones at typical broadband ~30s extra clone time per run × 4 agents × 6 runs/day = 12 minutes/day pure overhead.

primary_option.name: Extend TARGETS regex (smallest delta)
primary_option.change: Change the monitor/integrity entry from `globs: [/^report-.*\.json$/]` to `globs: [/^report-.*\.json$/, /^narrative-cite-audit-.*\.json$/, /^verify-pending-run-.*\.json$/]`. Optionally also add monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs subdirectory with `/^run-.*\.json$/` (363 files, also unmanaged).
primary_option.loc_change: 1 line modified, optionally a 4-line subdirectory entry added
primary_option.blast_radius: Script behavior only. Does not change agent prompts. Does not modify FUSE workspace state. The script is invocation-gated (--apply required for actual deletion).
primary_option.scheduling_question: Operator should decide: (a) leave script manual-invocation, run periodically by hand; (b) add a daily/weekly cron entry; (c) call from workspace-sync as a Step 3.7 with conservative retention (e.g., 30 days).

alternative_options.name: Skip-on-no-change in audit-narrative-citations.js
alternative_options.rationale: Hourly grep across recent workspace-sync agent_notes shows narrative-cite uncited has been stuck at 3296→3304 (variation < 0.3%) for at least 7 days, and bogus_anchors flat at 48. The hourly per-file payload is therefore ~99.9% redundant. Modifying the audit script to compare against the most recent file and SKIP writing if (uncited_count, bogus_count, file_count) is unchanged would reduce write rate ~40x.
alternative_options.blast_radius: Modifies audit-narrative-citations.js. Larger diff. Loses per-hour timestamp continuity (gaps when nothing changes). Need to either keep a 'heartbeat' file or accept gaps.
alternative_options.trade_off_vs_primary: Stronger long-term solution but bigger change. Combines well with primary option (rotation handles the residual).

alternative_options.name: Compact format (PROP-022-class architectural fix)
alternative_options.rationale: Each file repeats the same 1402 surface entries with same 1KB metadata each. A compact format would store one row per surface in a single rolling JSONL, with each file containing only DELTAS from the prior state.
alternative_options.blast_radius: Largest. Schema change to audit-narrative-citations.js + any consumer of the per-run file (currently only the workspace-sync soft-complaint grep, which only reads totals). Better fit for PROP-022's archive convention than a standalone PROP.
alternative_options.trade_off_vs_primary: Best long-term hygiene but high investment and overlaps with PROP-022 if/when that ships.

proposed_change.fuse_side_note: build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js explicitly cannot unlink FUSE files (FUSE rejects unlink). After this fix, FUSE will continue to grow and require periodic operator manual cleanup (file-tool delete works; bash unlink does not). This is a known limitation called out in the script header and in CLAUDE.md's two-repo-architecture section. Out of scope for this PROP.

verification_pattern.how_to_check_implemented: After change, `cd <clone> && node build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js --days 30` should report N candidate files including narrative-cite-audit-*.json from before 2026-04-14. Dry-run mode by default — no actual deletion until --apply.
verification_pattern.regression_risk: Low. Script is invocation-only; existing call sites (none scheduled) continue to behave as before. Worst-case undo is git revert of the build-scripts diff.
verification_pattern.verify_no_consumer_breakage: grep for `narrative-cite-audit` and `verify-pending-run` consumers across monitor/prompts/ confirms only producer (audit-narrative-citations.js) and one passive grepper (workspace-sync.md soft-complaint scan) reference these files. The grepper reads CURRENT-run file via `MECH3_*` variables in workspace-sync.md and would not be affected by deletion of older files.

counter_arguments.objection: Forensic value — these files trace when uncited count first crossed thresholds.
counter_arguments.response: Git log retains the deletions. Per-run uncited/bogus totals are also captured in workspace-sync agent_notes (search-grep-able). Information loss is bounded to per-paragraph surface samples, which are uniform across runs by design.

counter_arguments.objection: If the actual issue is /tmp/edit-clone (450MB, the disk-pressure trigger), this PROP doesn't address it.
counter_arguments.response: Correct — /tmp/edit-clone is operator's working clone and per the disk-cleanup empowerment is excluded if mtime < 24h. This PROP addresses the LONG-TERM accumulation (36MB/day, structural) which dominates the steady-state problem even when /tmp is empty. The two are independent fixes.

counter_arguments.objection: Why not just delete the files directly this run?
counter_arguments.response: (1) Tinker is not in the OWNERSHIP for build-scripts/ self-fixes at this scope without a PROP gate. (2) The FUSE side cannot be cleaned via tinker bash. (3) Operator may prefer skip-on-no-change (alternative option) or compaction (PROP-022-class) over rotation; PROP enables that choice.

counter_arguments.objection: Schedule the script to auto-run instead of leaving it manual.
counter_arguments.response: Reasonable, but cross-cuts with workflow questions (which clone runs it? what cadence? add to existing agent vs. new cron?). Punted to operator decision under primary_option.scheduling_question.

open_questions_for_operator: Q1: Primary fix only (extend regex), or pair with skip-on-no-change in audit-narrative-citations.js?
Q2: Retention window — keep default 14 days, or set narrative-cite to 30 days for forensic margin?
Q3: Schedule the script (where?) or leave manual?
Q4: Also include the workspace-sync-runs/ subdirectory (363 files of 1-3KB each — small bytes, big file-count)?
Q5: Do we need a parallel mechanism for FUSE-side cleanup, or accept that operator-manual is the answer (matches the rest of the pipeline)?
tinker_self_apply_recommendation: DO NOT self-apply. The change is small but pipeline-behavior-affecting and crosses several decision boundaries (retention, scheduling, mechanism). Operator owns the trade-offs.
